items.headline: The Iran war spills over into the U.S. economy: Inflation rises and growth slows.
items.summary: The conflict with Iran has already put fresh stress on the U.S. economy, as companies report rising prices, fewer orders and a decline in employment.

items.headline: Moody's cuts rating on private credit fund run by KKR and Future Standard to junk as bad loans grow
items.summary: The move by Moody's is the latest sign of distress in private credit; retail investors have been rushing to withdraw funds, from Blackstone to Apollo.

items.headline: European stocks dip as Iran war uncertainty lingers
items.summary: Shares listed in Europe dipped on Tuesday afternoon, as investors continue to monitor developments in the Middle East.

items.headline: 10-year Treasury yields edge higher as investors weigh renewed Iran war uncertainty
items.summary: The 10-year Treasury yield rose on Tuesday as renewed volatility in oil markets and lingering Middle East tensions kept investors on edge.

items.headline: FedEx launches same-day delivery with OneRail as Amazon, Walmart boost their speeds
items.summary: FedEx announced it's partnering with last-mile delivery company OneRail to bring same-day delivery to all of its customers.

items.headline: India’s private sector growth slows to over 3-year low in March, weighed down by Mideast conflict
items.summary: India's private-sector business activity, which had been on an upswing since the start of 2026, falters in March due to shockwaves from the Iran war

items.headline: Oil rises with Brent climbing back above $100 as optimism fades over Iran war de-escalation
items.summary: Oil prices traded higher on Tuesday, with international benchmark Brent crude climbing back above $100 per barrel.

items.headline: America is being haunted by a 1970s bogeyman known as stagflation. Here’s how big the threat is.
items.summary: In the 1970s, soaring inflation — along with misguided efforts by Washington, D.C., to control it — wreaked havoc on the economy. In just under a decade, from 1973 to 1982, the U.S. suffered three severe recessions.

items.headline: Fed rate hikes are a worry as Iran conflict drags on — but borrowing is already more expensive
items.summary: The uptick in borrowing costs can be traced to higher bond yields since the Iran conflict began in late February, but also to the large financing needs of the AI buildout

items.headline: Gold prices are steady after heavy sell-off
items.summary: The spot gold price began to settle but remained subdued on Tuesday after a tumultuous start to the week, establishing a level around $4,370 per troy ounce.

items.headline: These private-credit funds are giving back less than half the money their investors want
items.summary: Shares of Apollo Global Management and Ares Management dropped after private-credit funds the firms managed disclosed receiving more than double the redemption requests that the funds were willing to honor.

items.headline: ‘It feels slimy’: My friend offered to be my adviser, but didn’t tell me he’s paid to push financial products. Can I trust him?
items.summary: “His revenue sharing, which he did not disclose, creates conflicts of interest as advisers may be incentivized to recommend funds that pay them more.

items.headline: Iran attacks in Strait of Hormuz are 'economic terrorism against every nation,' UAE oil CEO says
items.summary: Sultan Ahmed Al Jaber, CEO of the UAE's state-owned oil company, said the Strait of Hormuz must reopen to resolve the supply shock.

items.headline: Flights will keep getting more expensive and harder to find. Here’s how much worse it could get.
items.summary: Travelers are struggling to score deals on airfares during an already chaotic travel season, as airlines cut flight routes and raise prices to combat rising jet-fuel prices.

items.headline: Donating from your IRA already has tax advantages. A bipartisan bill would expand retirees' options

items.headline: Gap says it will launch checkout within Google's Gemini, in an AI first from a major fashion company

items.summary: Shares of Puig Brands soared as much as 15% Tuesday after beauty peer Estée Lauder confirmed it is in talks about merging the two companies.

items.headline: Japan reports lower-than-expected core inflation for February, headline CPI eases for a fourth month
items.summary: The consumer price index fell to 1.3% last month, its lowest level since March 2022 and below the central bank's 2% target. It was down from 1.5% in January.

items.summary: The withdrawals show that Apollo didn't avoid the rush of investor redemptions plaguing rivals, driven by concern over private credit loans to software firms.
